steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how the narrator characterizes the Seine. Find the passage's contrast: the river is blocked and turbulent inside Paris, then spreads out and becomes peaceful in the countryside.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘As the journey progressed from Paris to the countryside, the waters of the Seine gradually cleared.’: Wrong. Water clarity is never mentioned. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The journey along the Seine is mostly peaceful, but the current intensifies briefly under the Point du Jour.’: Wrong. This reverses what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The waters of the Seine are more confined and rough in Paris than they are in the countryside.’: Correct. "Confined" fits the viaduct blocking the river; "rough" fits the contrast with the peaceful countryside river. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘The Seine's loveliness is surpassed only by that of Paris.’: Wrong. The narrator's language favors the countryside. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
